seoTitle: How Much Does Microsoft Excel Cost in 2026
metaDescription: Compare Microsoft Excel pricing across Microsoft 365 Personal and Family subscriptions, one-time Office purchases, business tiers, and free options.
intro: Excel's pricing spans everything from a free browser-based version to enterprise volume licensing, and picking the wrong tier means overpaying or missing features you need. This guide breaks down personal subscriptions, one-time purchase options, business seat pricing, and the legitimate ways to use Excel for free.

steps.heading: Check the Personal subscription rate
steps.body: Review the standard monthly Microsoft 365 Personal subscription cost for a single user.

steps.heading: Compare the Family plan
steps.body: Look at the multi-user Family package to see how splitting several account profiles lowers the cost per person.

steps.heading: Evaluate a one-time purchase
steps.body: Check the permanent Office Home edition for a flat, one-time lifetime license fee instead of a subscription.

steps.heading: Review business tiers
steps.body: Compare monthly seat costs across Microsoft 365 Business Basic, Standard, and Premium depending on whether you need offline desktop apps.

steps.heading: Look at free alternatives
steps.body: Consider using Excel Online with a basic Microsoft account, or check if you qualify for free academic access.

steps.heading: Check for volume licensing
steps.body: For large organizations, look into specialized custom volume licensing pathways instead of individual seats.

faq.question: Is a one-time purchase cheaper than a subscription long-term?
faq.answer: It depends on how long you plan to use it, since a one-time license avoids recurring fees but doesn't include ongoing feature updates that subscriptions provide.

faq.question: Can students get Excel for free?
faq.answer: Yes, students and teachers with a verified school domain email can often unlock the desktop apps at no cost.

faq.question: What's included in Business Basic versus Business Standard?
faq.answer: Business Basic is generally web-only access, while Business Standard includes full local offline desktop software installations.

faq.question: Is the web version of Excel really free?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use Excel Online for free simply by logging in with a basic Microsoft account through your browser.
